scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: AI policy for schools. [pause] GDPR, data privacy, and acceptable use.

scenes.name: Why
scenes.text: Every school now needs a written AI policy. [pause] Students use AI. Teachers use AI. Vendors pitch AI. [pause] In the absence of policy — every individual makes their own rules. That is not governance.

scenes.name: GDPR
scenes.text: Start with <emphasis>GDPR</emphasis>. [pause] Purpose limitation. Data minimisation. Storage limitation. [pause] When student data goes into a general AI tool — it leaves the school's control. That requires a lawful basis.

scenes.name: Components
scenes.text: Six components produce a workable policy. [pause] Scope. Permitted uses. Data handling. Tool approval. Disclosure. Review.

scenes.name: Permitted
scenes.text: The practical core — permitted and prohibited uses. [pause] Teachers may use AI on anonymised material. They may not paste identifiable student data into unapproved tools. [pause] Clarity protects everyone.

scenes.name: Approval
scenes.text: Tool approval matters. [pause] Before any new AI tool touches student data — review documentation, sign a Data Processing Agreement, complete an impact assessment. [pause] No agreement means no compliance.

scenes.name: Age
scenes.text: Student use is calibrated to age. [pause] Primary — restricted. Lower secondary — supervised. Upper secondary — structured individual use. [pause] Assessment is always treated separately.

scenes.name: Practice
scenes.text: A policy becomes real through three things. [pause] Active communication — not just publication. Practical infrastructure that supports the rules. And consistent enforcement — in every decision, not just after incidents.
